info.title: Configuration Service
info.description: Device/PV registry for Bluesky Remote Architecture (SVC-004). Plans are managed by Experiment Execution Service.

get.description:
Health check: state loaded AND registry DB queryable.

Runs ``SELECT 1`` against the registry store (when DB mode is on)
and returns 503 with the failure detail when it can't. Without
the DB ping, a mount-gone or permissions-revoked store would
still pass health while every CRUD call 500'd.
Readiness check endpoint.
Query available devices from registry

get.description: Return device-state changes after a given audit cursor. Intended for clients (like bluesky-queueserver) that cache device state and need to keep it fresh without re-fetching the full registry. Each device is reported at most once with its current state. If reset_occurred is true, the caller should discard local state and re-fetch /devices-info; likewise if service_epoch differs from a previously-observed value.

parameters.name: since_version

schema.description: Return changes with audit id greater than this value
schema.title: Since Version

parameters.description: Return changes with audit id greater than this value

post.description:
Acquire locks on multiple devices atomically (all-or-nothing).

When a device is locked, all PVs belonging to that device are implicitly
locked. Direct Control checks PV/device status before every write operation
and will refuse to command locked PVs.

Locks are held for the duration of a Bluesky plan (minutes to hours).
EE sends unlock when the plan completes, fails, or is aborted.

409.description: One or more devices already locked

404.description: One or more devices not found

422.description: One or more devices are disabled

post.description:
Release locks on devices. Only the item_id that acquired the lock can
release it. Use force-unlock for admin override.

Extend the lease on locks held by ``item_id`` (heartbeat).

Called periodically by the lock holder while it still needs the
devices. Only meaningful when leases are enabled
(CONFIG_LOCK_LEASE_TTL_SECONDS > 0); with leases disabled every held
lock is renewed as a no-op. ``lost_devices`` tells the holder which
locks it must re-acquire (expired, released, or dropped by a restart),
and ``lock_epoch`` confirms whether the authority itself reset.

This route is registered before the ``{device_name}`` wildcard so
``renew`` is never matched as a device name.

Emergency endpoint to clear stale locks regardless of ownership.

Use when EE crashes mid-plan and locks are orphaned. Requires ADMIN
role when AuthZ middleware is enabled.

get.description: Current lock_all availability policy (boot default: CONFIG_LOCK_ALL).

put.description:
Set the lock_all availability policy at runtime.

Takes effect immediately for every subsequent availability read
(device status, PV status). In-memory like the locks themselves —
a restart returns to the CONFIG_LOCK_ALL boot default.

get.description:
Combined availability check: lock state + enabled/disabled + PV health.

A device is available only when it is both enabled and unlocked.
``pv_health`` is a dict keyed by PV name; only PVs with reported
caput outcomes appear, so an empty dict means "no failures
observed yet for any of this device's PVs".

post.description: Direct-control calls this after a caput fails (EPICS timeout, put-rejection, IOC unreachable, etc.). Increments the PV's consecutive-failure counter and updates last_failure_at. The ``message`` body field carries the diagnostic the operator UI shows next to the unhealthy PV. Returns 404 if ``pv_name`` is not registered.

Direct-control calls this after a caput succeeds. If a record exists for the PV (i.e. it had previously failed), resets the consecutive-failure counter to zero and flips the state back to ``healthy`` — a recent success is stronger evidence than older failures. For PVs that have never failed since service start, the response carries a synthetic healthy record but no record is persisted (keeps the health store bounded by the PVs that have actually failed, not every PV ever caput'd). Returns 404 if ``pv_name`` is not registered.

get.description: Returns the current health record for ``pv_name``. 404 if no failures have been recorded for this PV (i.e. either it has only succeeded since service start, or never been written to at all). Successes on never-failed PVs are intentionally not persisted, so a 404 here does not mean 'never touched' — frontends should treat it as 'no failures observed, assume healthy'.

delete.description: Idempotently drops the in-memory health record for ``pv_name``. Useful when an operator wants to ack a PV that's been marked ``unresponsive`` by past failures so the UI no longer shows the warning. Returns ``cleared: 1`` if a record was actually removed, ``cleared: 0`` if no record existed (still 200, idempotent).
Wipes every in-memory health record. Returns the count of records removed. Intended for ops use (e.g. after IOC maintenance) — there's no per-PV gate, so call deliberately.

get.description: Returns the total tracked PV count plus a per-state count (healthy / degraded / unresponsive). Every state appears as a key in ``by_state`` even if its count is zero, so callers never have to special-case missing keys.

patch.description: Enable a device so it will be instantiated by remote services

get.description:
Check whether a PV can be commanded (caput).

Resolves PV to its owning device and returns the device's lock and
enabled state. Standalone PVs (not bound to a device) are always
available. This is the primary endpoint Direct Control calls before
every write operation.
Get detailed metadata for specific PV
Look up nested device component by dot-separated path

post.summary: Resolve Dotted Device Addresses to PV Names
post.description: Walk the ophyd / ophyd-async device class for each address and return the underlying EPICS PV. Read-only, best-effort per-item — no halt-on-error. Used by the frontend to translate friendly addresses like 'vortex.mca.rois.roi2.lo_chan' into the PV strings needed by direct-control's batch caput.

DeviceAuditEntry.title: DeviceAuditEntry
DeviceAuditEntry.description: Entry in the device audit log (append-only change history).

DeviceCRUDResponse.title: DeviceCRUDResponse
DeviceCRUDResponse.description: Response model for device CRUD operations.

version.title: Version
version.description: Audit log id of the change that produced this state

DeviceChangeEntry.title: DeviceChangeEntry
DeviceChangeEntry.description:
One device's current state, reported in a delta response.

``op`` is "upsert" if the device currently exists (create or update), or
"delete" if it no longer exists. For "upsert", ``metadata`` and ``spec``
reflect the current state; for "delete" both are null.

current_version.title: Current Version
current_version.description: Latest audit log id at query time

reset_occurred.title: Reset Occurred
reset_occurred.description: True if a registry-wide reset happened in the range

DeviceChangesResponse.title: DeviceChangesResponse
DeviceChangesResponse.description:
Delta response: every device whose state changed after ``since_version``.

If ``reset_occurred`` is true, a registry-wide reset happened in the
requested range and the caller should discard its local state and
re-fetch the full registry instead of applying ``changes`` incrementally.
``service_epoch`` is a stable identifier that changes only on manual
re-seed or DB wipe; if it differs from the value the caller saw last,
treat the cursor as invalid.

DeviceCreateRequest.title: DeviceCreateRequest
DeviceCreateRequest.description: Request model for creating a runtime device.

example.reason: EE crashed during rel_scan, clearing stale locks

active.title: Active
active.description: Whether this device should be instantiated

DeviceInstantiationSpec.title: DeviceInstantiationSpec
DeviceInstantiationSpec.description:
Device instantiation specification for remote device creation.

This model contains all information needed to recreate a device instance
in another service (e.g., Experiment Execution Service). By providing
the class path and constructor arguments, remote services can dynamically
import and instantiate identical device objects.

This enables Configuration Service to be the single source of truth for
device definitions, ensuring PV names and configurations are consistent
across all services.

active.title: Active
active.description: Whether this device should be instantiated

DeviceInstantiationSpecUpdate.title: DeviceInstantiationSpecUpdate
DeviceInstantiationSpecUpdate.description:
Partial of DeviceInstantiationSpec for PATCH/PUT updates.

Every field is Optional with default None so callers can send only
the fields they want to change. Pair with ``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)``
to distinguish "not sent" from "sent as None". Field set must mirror
DeviceInstantiationSpec — enforced by test_partial_models_field_parity.

DeviceLabel.title: DeviceLabel
DeviceLabel.description:
Device classification derived from ophyd/ophyd-async class hierarchy.

Each value maps directly to a concrete base class in ophyd or ophyd-async:
  MOTOR     — ophyd.EpicsMotor, ophyd_async.epics.motor.Motor
  DETECTOR  — ophyd.areadetector.DetectorBase, ophyd_async.core.StandardDetector
  SIGNAL    — ophyd.Signal/EpicsSignal, ophyd_async.core.Signal
  FLYER     — ophyd.FlyerInterface, ophyd_async.core.StandardFlyer
  READABLE  — ophyd_async.core.StandardReadable (readable but not motor/detector)
  DEVICE    — ophyd.Device, ophyd_async.core.Device (generic base)

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the lock failed (not_found, disabled, already_locked)

DeviceLockConflict.title: DeviceLockConflict
DeviceLockConflict.description: A single device that could not be locked.

item_id.title: Item Id
item_id.description: Queue item ID that holds the lock

DeviceLockRenewRequest.title: DeviceLockRenewRequest
DeviceLockRenewRequest.description: Request model for renewing (heartbeating) held device locks.

success.title: Success
success.description: True only if every device was renewed

lost_devices.title: Lost Devices
lost_devices.description: Requested devices no longer held here — re-acquire needed

conflict_devices.title: Conflict Devices
conflict_devices.description: Requested devices currently held by a different item_id

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: New lease expiry (null when leases disabled)

DeviceLockRenewResponse.title: DeviceLockRenewResponse
DeviceLockRenewResponse.description:
Response model for a lock-renewal (heartbeat).

``success`` is True only when every requested device was renewed. When
``lost`` is non-empty the holder no longer owns those locks here (they
expired, were released, or the authority restarted) and must re-acquire;
``lock_epoch`` lets the holder confirm an authority reset.

item_id.title: Item Id
item_id.description: Queue item ID holding the lock

plan_name.title: Plan Name
plan_name.description: Name of the plan acquiring devices

DeviceLockRequest.title: DeviceLockRequest
DeviceLockRequest.description: Request model for acquiring device locks (bulk atomic).

lock_epoch.title: Lock Epoch
lock_epoch.description: Lock-authority generation id. Changes when configuration_service restarts (in-memory lock state is rebuilt). Holders compare this across calls to detect that their locks were dropped and must be re-acquired.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: ISO timestamp when the lease lapses if not renewed, or null when leases are disabled (CONFIG_LOCK_LEASE_TTL_SECONDS=0).

lease_ttl_seconds.title: Lease Ttl Seconds
lease_ttl_seconds.description: Configured lease TTL in seconds (0 = leases disabled).

DeviceLockResponse.title: DeviceLockResponse
DeviceLockResponse.description: Response model for successful lock acquisition.

module.title: Module
module.description: Python module containing the device class

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Parent device if this is a component

DeviceMetadata.title: DeviceMetadata
DeviceMetadata.description:
Device metadata model.

Represents device information loaded from profile collections.
Maps to ProvidesDeviceRegistry.get_device() return type.

Compatible with ophyd/ophyd-async device introspection and profile collection formats.

module.title: Module
module.description: Python module containing the device class

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Parent device if this is a component

DeviceMetadataUpdate.title: DeviceMetadataUpdate
DeviceMetadataUpdate.description:
Partial of DeviceMetadata for PATCH/PUT updates.

Every field is Optional with default None so callers can send only
the fields they want to change. Pair with ``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)``
to distinguish "not sent" from "sent as None". Field set must mirror
DeviceMetadata — enforced by test_partial_models_field_parity.

available.title: Available
available.description: True only when enabled AND unlocked

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether the device is enabled for instantiation

locked_by_item.title: Locked By Item
locked_by_item.description: Queue item ID holding the lock

locked_until.title: Locked Until
locked_until.description: ISO timestamp when the current lock's lease lapses if not renewed, or null when unlocked or leases are disabled.

lock_epoch.title: Lock Epoch
lock_epoch.description: Lock-authority generation id. Changes on a configuration_service restart (in-memory lock state is rebuilt). Readers (direct-control) can detect that the lock table was reset — every device will report unlocked until holders re-acquire.

pv_health.title: Pv Health
pv_health.description: PV-level health records keyed by PV name. Only PVs that have failed at least once since service start appear here — PVs with no failures (or only successes) are intentionally not persisted to keep the store bounded by 'PVs needing attention' rather than 'PVs ever caput'd'. Absence therefore means 'no failures observed, assume healthy'.

DeviceStatusResponse.title: DeviceStatusResponse
DeviceStatusResponse.description: Combined device availability check (lock + enabled state + PV health).

item_id.title: Item Id
item_id.description: Queue item ID that holds the lock

DeviceUnlockRequest.title: DeviceUnlockRequest
DeviceUnlockRequest.description: Request model for releasing device locks.

metadata.description: Partial device metadata — only included fields are updated

instantiation_spec.description: Partial instantiation spec — only included fields are updated

DeviceUpdateRequest.title: DeviceUpdateRequest
DeviceUpdateRequest.description:
Request model for updating a device.

Supports field-level partial updates: only the fields you include
in ``metadata`` or ``instantiation_spec`` are changed.  Omitted fields
keep their current values.

LockPolicy.title: LockPolicy
LockPolicy.description:
Global device-lock availability policy.

``lock_all=True``: while ANY device lock is held (a plan is running),
every registered device reports locked/unavailable — not just the
devices the plan named. Lock acquisition/release semantics are
unchanged. Boot default comes from CONFIG_LOCK_ALL; runtime value is
read/changed via GET/PUT /api/v1/devices/lock/policy.

NestedDeviceComponent.title: NestedDeviceComponent
NestedDeviceComponent.description: Information about a nested device component.

Outcome.title: Outcome
Outcome.description:
Per-address result kind.

``NEEDS_ENRICHMENT`` and ``ENRICHMENT_UNAVAILABLE`` are distinct: the
former means we found a runtime placeholder we can't fill in
statically *and* live-enrichment isn't configured for this deploy;
the latter means enrichment was attempted (via the direct-control
client) and the call failed (network, timeout, 5xx). Frontends can
branch — needs_enrichment is a deploy-config gap while
enrichment_unavailable is typically transient and worth a retry.

PVHealthClearResponse.title: PVHealthClearResponse
PVHealthClearResponse.description:
Response from the admin clear endpoints.

``cleared`` is the count of records actually removed: 0 or 1 for the
single-PV endpoint (idempotent on missing records), N for the
clear-all endpoint.

PVHealthRecord.title: PVHealthRecord
PVHealthRecord.description:
Per-PV health record, returned by the health endpoints + embedded
in the device-status response.

``state`` is a computed_field derived from ``consecutive_failures``;
callers should never construct a record with an inconsistent state.

message.title: Message
message.description: Diagnostic message for failure reports (EPICS error, timeout reason, etc.).

PVHealthReport.title: PVHealthReport
PVHealthReport.description:
Request body for the failure/success report endpoints from direct-control.

Only failure reports carry a message; success reports are zero-content
beyond the URL path identifying the PV.

PVHealthState.title: PVHealthState
PVHealthState.description:
Per-PV health state derived from recent caput outcomes.

Direct-control reports each caput's outcome (success or failure) back
to configuration_service. The state is computed from the consecutive-
failure counter so a single successful caput always resets to healthy.

Frontends should color-code by state and surface ``unresponsive`` PVs
prominently — they typically mean the IOC is down or a PV has been
deleted from it.

PVHealthStateCounts.title: PVHealthStateCounts
PVHealthStateCounts.description:
Per-state PV-health record counts.

Three explicit fields (one per :class:`PVHealthState` value) rather
than ``Dict[str, int]`` so the OpenAPI schema actually enforces the
"every state is always present" contract — generated SDK clients
get proper accessors and can't drift on a future state-machine
addition without updating this model in lockstep.

by_state.description: Count of records grouped by state. All three states are always present as fields, zero if no records match.

tracked_pvs.title: Tracked Pvs
tracked_pvs.description: Total number of PVs with at least one health record.

PVHealthStats.title: PVHealthStats
PVHealthStats.description:
At-a-glance count of PV-health records grouped by state.

``tracked_pvs`` is a ``computed_field`` derived from ``by_state``,
so the two can never drift out of sync.

PVMetadata.title: PVMetadata
PVMetadata.description:
EPICS PV metadata model.

Represents PV information from EPICS network discovery.
Maps to ProvidesDeviceRegistry.get_pv_metadata() return type.

PVProtocol.title: PVProtocol
PVProtocol.description: EPICS protocol for standalone PV access.

available.title: Available
available.description: True when owning device is enabled and unlocked (or standalone)

device_name.title: Device Name
device_name.description: Owning device name (null for standalone PVs)

device_enabled.title: Device Enabled
device_enabled.description: Whether the owning device is enabled

locked_by_plan.title: Locked By Plan
locked_by_plan.description: Plan holding the lock on the owning device

locked_by_item.title: Locked By Item
locked_by_item.description: Queue item ID holding the lock

PVStatusResponse.title: PVStatusResponse
PVStatusResponse.description: PV availability check (resolves PV to owning device lock state).

PathResolveRequest.title: PathResolveRequest
PathResolveRequest.description:
Batch request to resolve dotted device addresses to PV names.

Each address is one of:
- ``"<device>"`` — top-level happi entry. For classic-ophyd entries
  whose class is a single ``EpicsSignal`` / ``EpicsMotor`` this
  resolves to the device's prefix. ophyd-async devices always need a
  sub-attribute and return ``no_such_attr`` for top-level addressing.
- ``"<device>.<attr>.<attr>..."`` — walks the device class structure.

Resolution never opens EPICS connections, but the per-framework
introspection differs:

- **Classic ophyd**: configuration_service walks the class-level
  ``Component`` / ``DynamicDeviceComponent`` / ``FormattedComponent``
  declarations. No instantiation. ``FormattedComponent`` suffixes
  with real ``{}`` placeholders return ``needs_enrichment`` since
  they need a live parent to evaluate.
- **ophyd-async**: signals are created in ``__init__``, so
  configuration_service instantiates the device locally (without
  calling ``.connect()``) and reads each leaf signal's
  ``.source`` URI.

PathResolveResponse.title: PathResolveResponse
PathResolveResponse.description:
Aggregate response for a batch resolve.

Always 200 — per-address outcomes are in the rows. Resolution is
read-only with no state change, so "best effort with per-item errors"
is the right semantic; unlike batch caput there's no halt-on-failure.

outcome.description: Per-address result kind. See ``PathResolveOutcome`` for the full set of values.

PathResolveResultItem.title: PathResolveResultItem
PathResolveResultItem.description:
One row in the batch response.

``ok`` is derived from ``outcome`` (``True`` iff
``outcome is Outcome.RESOLVED``); declared as a ``computed_field`` so
the JSON output carries it for client convenience but it cannot drift
from ``outcome``. Other outcomes carry their reason in ``message`` so
the frontend can show the operator why a particular address couldn't
be resolved.

StandalonePV.title: StandalonePV
StandalonePV.description: A standalone PV not associated with any ophyd device.

StandalonePVCRUDResponse.title: StandalonePVCRUDResponse
StandalonePVCRUDResponse.description: Response model for standalone PV CRUD operations.

pv_name.title: Pv Name
pv_name.description: EPICS PV name (non-empty, printable ASCII, no whitespace)

StandalonePVCreateRequest.title: StandalonePVCreateRequest
StandalonePVCreateRequest.description: Request model for registering a standalone PV.

StandalonePVUpdateRequest.title: StandalonePVUpdateRequest
StandalonePVUpdateRequest.description:
Request model for updating a standalone PV.

All fields optional.  Only fields included in the request body are
applied; omitted fields keep their current values.
